response: The global food system faces unprecedented pressure as the world population is expected to reach 9.7 billion by 2050, requiring about a 60% increase in food production. Climate change is already lowering yields in many regions, while agriculture accounts for roughly 25% of greenhouse gas emissions and water scarcity—such as the rapid depletion of the Ogallala Aquifer—threatens major farming areas. Soil degradation has reduced productivity on 33% of the world’s arable land, and around one-third of food is lost or wasted between farm and fork. Proposed responses include precision agriculture (AI/sensors), vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant crops (including genetic modification), and regenerative farming, along with supportive policy changes to scale solutions while keeping food affordable for the poorest.
